snippets: Hotels are available in Amberg.
Hotels are available in the city centre of Amberg.
Hotels are available in the surrounding area of Amberg.
Holiday apartments are available in Amberg.
Guesthouses are available in Amberg.
Accommodation can be booked through the official tourism portal.
Online booking is available under the category Übernachtung.
Hotel Brunner is located on Batteriegasse in the old town of Amberg.
Hotel Brunner is situated on cobblestone streets.
Hotel Brunner is located near a bronze sculpture.
Hotel Brunner is located near a tree known as the Götterbaum.
The Hotel Brunner building dates from the 1950s.
Hotel Brunner features trees growing from balconies.
The design of the balcony trees is inspired by Hundertwasser.
Hotel Brunner has a spiral staircase.
The staircase steps are made from Italian marble.
The walls display paintings of Amberg’s history.
Rooms in Hotel Brunner are named instead of numbered.
Room names include Melonenzimmer.
Room names include Kontrollraum.
Room names include Hohlraum.
Rooms are decorated by local artists.
Artists include Heini Hohl.
Artists include Regina Uber.
Artists include Marcus Trepesch.
Hotel Brunner offers a pillow bar.
The pillow bar is stored in a handcrafted cabinet.
Hotel Brunner has a breakfast room called Mariahilf-Stüberl.
The breakfast includes freshly ground coffee.
Rooms include a minibar.
Hotel Brunner has an underground parking garage.
Electric car charging is available in the garage.
A bicycle garage is available for guests.
The bicycle garage includes cleaning equipment.
Guests can borrow bicycles for free.
Hotel Brunner has an adjacent bar called Atelier Teufelsbäck.
Atelier Teufelsbäck serves homemade food.
Atelier Teufelsbäck offers vegan options.
Atelier Teufelsbäck includes a beer garden.
Atelier Teufelsbäck has a small live performance stage.
Live concerts are held multiple times per year.
Hotel Brunner allows cats in rooms.
E-bike charging is available at Hotel Brunner.
Hotel Drahthammerschlössl is located on the outskirts of Amberg.
The hotel is part of a historic complex.
The site has a history of over 1,000 years.
The location was possibly used as a court gathering place before the year 1000.
The site was documented as novum molendinum around 1280.
The site refers to a mill known as Neumühle.
The mill may date back to the year 1034.
The mill was associated with Emperor Konrad II.
The mill was connected to the Bishop of Bamberg.
The mill dam regulated river shipping.
The miller controlled water levels for transport.
The dam was closed after ships returned.
A wire-drawing facility existed at the site from 1413.
Wire masters worked at the site from 1475.
Iron rods were produced using hammering techniques.
Wire production used both local iron and Styrian steel.
Styrian steel was known for its flexibility.
The name Drahthammer replaced Neumühle.
The site was reacquired by the city in 1559.
Wire production ended after reacquisition.
The complex was taken over by the Trettenbach family in 1986.
The complex was fully restored.
The site was converted into a hotel and restaurant.
The hotel features elegant interiors.
The hotel includes multiple restaurant areas.
The hotel serves regional and international cuisine.
The hotel offers high-end culinary experiences.
The hotel emphasizes exceptional service.
Hotel Fronfeste is located in a former district court prison.
The prison is called Fronfeste.
The prison has a history of 300 years.
The hotel concept is based on a prison theme.
Guests sleep in former prison cells.
Rooms retain original structural elements.
Rooms are modernized while preserving historical character.
Guests are welcomed by staff referred to as wardens.
The hotel offers an open custody sleeping concept.
The building has housed prisoners since at least the 17th century.
The building was renovated with heritage preservation standards.
The renovation received a heritage award.
The award is the Denkmalpreis des Bezirks Oberpfalz.
The hotel is certified as a Bett+Bike accommodation.
The hotel provides secure bicycle storage.
E-bike charging is available at the hotel.
The hotel is suitable for meetings and events.
The hotel includes an escape room.
Guests must solve puzzles to exit the escape room.
The food concept includes themed offerings like bread and water.
The hotel offers a breakfast buffet.
Hotel Eh’häusl is located in Seminargasse.
Hotel Eh’häusl is located in the old town of Amberg.
The building is only 2.5 meters wide.
Hotel Eh’häusl dates back to 1728.
The building was constructed to meet marriage property requirements.
Couples needed property ownership to marry in 1728.
A merchant built the house in a narrow gap.
The house served as proof of property ownership.
Couples could purchase the property to marry.
The building was renovated in 2008.
The hotel is designed as a luxury hermitage.
The interior includes dynamic lighting effects.
Music is played inside the house.
The music includes Verdi’s La Traviata.
The hotel has no reception desk.
The hotel has no porter service.
The concept focuses on privacy.
The hotel can be rented as a whole unit.
The stay is designed as an exclusive experience.
Vienna House Easy by Wyndham is a modern hotel in Amberg.
The hotel is located near the Amberger Congress Centrum.
The hotel is located near the Kurfürstenbad.
The hotel is located near the former Landesgartenschau grounds.
Parking is available directly outside the hotel.
The old town is within walking distance.
The walking route passes under the Stadtbrille.
The route passes by the Luftmuseum.
Rooms feature bright colors.
Rooms include local design elements.
Rooms include boxspring beds.
The hotel has a bar.
The hotel offers a breakfast buffet.
The breakfast includes cereals and yogurt.
The breakfast includes fruits and juices.
The breakfast includes homemade jams.
The breakfast includes cold cuts and cheese.
The breakfast includes freshly brewed coffee.
Bicycles are available for hire.
Das Bootshaus is located in the old town of Amberg.
Das Bootshaus is located along the Vils river.
The hotel consists of five historic buildings.
Some buildings date back to the 13th century.
The buildings were part of the first Electoral Palace.
The buildings were restored under heritage protection.
The buildings were internally connected.
One building was previously used as a dance school.
The hotel has 19 rooms.
The hotel includes 15 double rooms.
The hotel includes 4 suites.
Each room has a unique design.
Rooms feature timber-frame walls.
Rooms include historic roof structures.
Rooms include exposed beams.
Rooms include round arches.
Rooms include freestanding bathtubs.
Rooms include hidden loggias.
All rooms have a connection to the Vils river.
The hotel includes a private library.
The hotel has a meeting room for up to 10 people.
The hotel has a banquet hall.
The banquet hall has Gothic windows.
The banquet hall overlooks the Vils.
The banquet hall can host up to 42 people.
Food is prepared fresh.
Ingredients are regional and seasonal.
The menu is small and curated.
The cuisine is based on Upper Palatinate traditions.
Breakfast is served at the table.
Guests can reorder breakfast items.
The name Bootshaus refers to boat traditions.
Allee Parkhotel Maximilian is located at the edge of the old town.
The hotel is adjacent to a park.
The hotel is family-run.
Rooms are named after locations in Amberg.
Room names include Zwinger.
Room names include Jesuitenfahrt.
Room names include Martinskirche.
The hotel offers multiple room types.
Room types include single rooms.
Room types include suites.
Some suites include a private whirlpool.
The hotel includes a terrace.
The hotel includes a sauna area.
The hotel includes a daytime bar.
The hotel has an underground parking garage.
Guests can start walking routes directly from the hotel.
Guests can start cycling routes directly from the hotel.
